-- Step 1: Create a lookup table for buildupplayspeedclass values
CREATE TABLE buildupplayspeedclass_lookup (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    value TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL
);

-- Step 2: Insert existing enum values plus the new 'Very Fast' value
INSERT INTO buildupplayspeedclass_lookup (value) 
VALUES ('Slow'), ('Balanced'), ('Fast'), ('Very Fast');

-- Step 3: Add a new column with foreign key reference
ALTER TABLE team_attributes 
ADD COLUMN buildupplayspeedclass_new INTEGER 
REFERENCES buildupplayspeedclass_lookup(id);

-- Step 4: Populate the new column with corresponding IDs
UPDATE team_attributes ta
SET buildupplayspeedclass_new = bl.id
FROM buildupplayspeedclass_lookup bl
WHERE ta.buildupplayspeedclass = bl.value;

-- Step 5: Drop the old enum type and column, rename the new column
ALTER TABLE team_attributes 
DROP COLUMN buildupplayspeedclass;

ALTER TABLE team_attributes 
RENAME COLUMN buildupplayspeedclass_new TO buildupplayspeedclass;

-- Step 6: Create index for better performance
CREATE INDEX idx_team_attributes_buildupplayspeedclass 
ON team_attributes(buildupplayspeedclass)

WITH user_groups AS (
    -- Create groups of related users based on shared refs
    SELECT 
        u1.id as base_id,
        u1.username,
        u1.ref1,
        u1.ref2,
        COALESCE(u1.ref1, u1.ref2) as group_key
    FROM users u1
),
merged_users AS (
    -- Handle the merging logic more carefully
    SELECT DISTINCT
        u1.id,
        u1.username,
        u1.ref1,
        u1.ref2,
        CASE 
            WHEN u1.ref1 IS NOT NULL THEN u1.ref1
            ELSE u1.ref2
        END as owner_ref
    FROM users u1
)
SELECT 
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT mu.id) AS ids,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT mu.username) AS usernames,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT mu.ref1) AS refs1,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT mu.ref2) AS refs2,
    COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS asset_count
FROM merged_users mu
LEFT JOIN assets a ON (a.owner = mu.ref1 OR a.owner = mu.ref2)
GROUP BY mu.owner_ref
ORDER BY MIN(mu.id)

(
  SELECT
    account.account_id,
    account.frequency,
    l.loan_id,
    l.date AS loan_date,
    0 AS priority
  FROM account
  LEFT JOIN loan l
    ON account.account_id = l.account_id
  WHERE account.district_id = '18'
    AND l.date >= (NOW() - INTERVAL '48 hours')
  ORDER BY l.date DESC NULLS LAST
  LIMIT 6
)
UNION
(
  SELECT
    account.account_id,
    account.frequency,
    l.loan_id,
    l.date AS loan_date,
    1 AS priority
  FROM account
  LEFT JOIN loan l
    ON account.account_id = l.account_id
  WHERE account.district_id = '18'
  EXCEPT
  SELECT
    account.account_id,
    account.frequency,
    l.loan_id,
    l.date AS loan_date,
    1 AS priority
  FROM account
  LEFT JOIN loan l
    ON account.account_id = l.account_id
  WHERE account.district_id = '18'
    AND l.date >= (NOW() - INTERVAL '48 hours')
  ORDER BY account.date DESC
)
ORDER BY priority, loan_date DESC NULLS LAST

SELECT 
    COUNT(DISTINCT releasedate) AS count,
    MIN(releasedate) AS "date MIN",
    MAX(releasedate) AS "date MAX"
FROM (
    SELECT 
        releasedate,
        releasedate - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY releasedate) * INTERVAL '1 day' AS grp
    FROM (
        SELECT DISTINCT releasedate
        FROM sets_releaseinfo
        WHERE releasedate IS NOT NULL
    ) distinct_dates
) grouped_dates
GROUP BY grp
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT releasedate) > 1
ORDER BY count DESC, MIN(releasedate)

WITH RECURSIVE path_cte AS (
  -- Anchor: Find starting cards (cards that have no previous card)
  SELECT id, nextcardid, id::TEXT AS path
  FROM collection
  WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT nextcardid FROM collection WHERE nextcardid IS NOT NULL)
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive: Follow the chain forward
  SELECT c.id, c.nextcardid, p.path || ' --> ' || c.id::TEXT
  FROM collection c
  JOIN path_cte p ON c.id = p.nextcardid
)
-- Select only the final paths in each chain
SELECT path
FROM path_cte p1
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 
  FROM path_cte p2 
  WHERE p2.path LIKE p1.path || ' --> %'
)
ORDER BY id

SELECT 
    cl.relname AS base_table,
    af.attname AS base_col,
    cr.relname AS referencing_table,
    ar.attname AS referencing_col,
    pg_get_constraintdef(c.oid) AS constraint_sql
FROM pg_constraint c
JOIN pg_class cl ON c.confrelid = cl.oid  -- referenced table (users)
JOIN pg_class cr ON c.conrelid = cr.oid   -- referencing table
JOIN pg_attribute af ON af.attrelid = c.confrelid AND af.attnum = ANY(c.confkey)  -- referenced columns
JOIN pg_attribute ar ON ar.attrelid = c.conrelid AND ar.attnum = ANY(c.conkey)    -- referencing columns
WHERE cl.relname = 'users'
  AND c.contype = 'f'  -- foreign key constraints only
ORDER BY referencing_table, referencing_col

    FROM trans

), 
two AS (
    SELECT date_trunc('year', date) as year, COUNT(*) as loan_count 
    FROM loan 
    GROUP BY date_trunc('year', date)
)
SELECT 
    COALESCE(one.year, two.year) as year,
    COALESCE(trans_count, 0) + COALESCE(loan_count, 0) as count
FROM one

WITH monthly_purchases AS (
    -- Get distinct months for each user
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        user_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_DATE(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD')) AS purchase_month
    FROM purchase
),
streak_groups AS (
    -- Assign group numbers to consecutive months
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        purchase_month,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY purchase_month) AS row_num,
        purchase_month - INTERVAL '1 month' * 
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY purchase_month) AS streak_group
    FROM monthly_purchases
),
streak_lengths AS (
    -- Calculate length of each streak
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        COUNT(*) AS streak_length
    FROM streak_groups
    GROUP BY user_id, streak_group
),
max_streaks AS (
    -- Get the maximum streak length for each user
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        MAX(streak_length) AS max_streak_length
    FROM streak_lengths
    GROUP BY user_id
)
-- Count how many users have each longest streak length
SELECT 
    max_streak_length AS streak_length,
    COUNT(*) AS num_users
FROM max_streaks
GROUP BY max_streak_length
ORDER BY streak_length DESC

WITH tab_with_cardid AS (
    SELECT 
        cardid,
        CASE 
            WHEN cardid LIKE '%-%-%' THEN
                SUBSTRING(cardid FROM 1 FOR POSITION('-' IN cardid)) || 
                SUBSTRING(cardid FROM POSITION('-' IN SUBSTRING(cardid FROM POSITION('-' IN cardid) + 1)) + POSITION('-' IN cardid))
            ELSE cardid
        END AS pure_cardid,
        type,
        last_refresh_date
    FROM card_info
), ranked_records AS (
    SELECT 
        cardid,
        pure_cardid,
        type,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY pure_cardid ORDER BY type) AS rn
    FROM tab_with_cardid
)
SELECT 
    cardid, 
    pure_cardid,
    type
FROM ranked_records
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY pure_cardid

SELECT json_agg(
    json_build_object(
        'id_module', p.id_module,
        'id_parent_module', p.id_parent_module,
        'module_code', p.module_code,
        'name_module', p.name_module,
        'desc_module', p.desc_module,
        'children', CASE 
            WHEN c.children IS NOT NULL THEN c.children 
            ELSE '[]'::json 
        END
    )
)::text AS result
FROM modules p
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        m.id_parent_module,
        json_agg(
            json_build_object(
                'id_module', m.id_module,
                'id_parent_module', m.id_parent_module,
                'module_code', m.module_code,
                'name_module', m.name_module,
                'desc_module', m.desc_module
            )
        ) AS children
    FROM modules m
    WHERE m.id_parent_module IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY m.id_parent_module
) c ON p.id_module = c.id_parent_module
WHERE p.id_parent_module IS NULL
ORDER BY p.id_module

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION f_gap_fill_update(tbl text, id_col text, order_col text) 
RETURNS void LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $func$
DECLARE 
    tmp text[];
    col text;
    sql_stmt text;
BEGIN
    -- Get all nullable columns for the specified table
    SELECT array_agg(column_name) 
    INTO tmp
    FROM information_schema.columns 
    WHERE table_name = tbl 
    AND is_nullable = 'YES'
    AND column_name NOT IN (id_col, order_col);
    
    -- Loop through each nullable column
    FOREACH col IN ARRAY tmp LOOP
        -- Build the UPDATE statement with forward fill logic
        sql_stmt := format('
            UPDATE %I t1
            SET %I = t2.%I
            FROM (
                SELECT %I, %I,
                    COALESCE(%I, 
                        LAG(%I) OVER (PARTITION BY %I ORDER BY %I)
                    ) AS filled_%I
                FROM %I
            ) t2
            WHERE t1.%I = t2.%I 
            AND t1.%I = t2.%I
            AND t1.%I IS NULL',
            tbl, col, col,
            id_col, order_col, col, col, id_col, order_col,
            col, id_col, order_col,
            id_col, id_col, order_col, order_col, col
        );
        
        -- Execute the dynamic SQL
        EXECUTE sql_stmt;
    END LOOP;
END;
$func$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.generate_series_monthly(a date, b date) 
RETURNS SETOF date 
LANGUAGE SQL 
IMMUTABLE PARALLEL SAFE 
ROWS 5 
AS $function$ 
SELECT generate_series(date_trunc('month', a), date_trunc('month', b), '1 month')
$function$;

-- Test the function - both queries should now work consistently
EXPLAIN SELECT generate_series_monthly('2024-01-01', '2024-05-01');
EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM generate_series_monthly('2024-01-01', '2024-05-01')

WITH match_with_draw_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        m.id,
        m.date,
        m.home_team_goal,
        m.away_team_goal,
        CASE WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS invoiced,
        SUM(CASE WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
            OVER (ORDER BY m.id, m.date ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS draw_group
    FROM match AS m
),
accumulated_goals AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        date,
        invoiced,
        home_team_goal,
        away_team_goal,
        SUM(COALESCE(home_team_goal, 0) + COALESCE(away_team_goal, 0)) 
            OVER (PARTITION BY draw_group ORDER BY id, date) AS running_total
    FROM match_with_draw_groups
)
SELECT 
    id,
    date,
    invoiced,
    running_total AS amount
FROM accumulated_goals
WHERE invoiced = 1
ORDER BY id, date

WITH LastTransactionPerStation AS (
  -- Get the last transaction for each customer-gasstation combination
  SELECT *
  FROM (
    SELECT *,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC) as rn
    FROM transactions_1k
  ) t
  WHERE rn = 1
),
CustomerIssueStations AS (
  -- Find the first gas station where each customer had amount < 10
  SELECT customerid,
         gasstationid as first_issue_gasstation
  FROM (
    SELECT customerid, gasstationid,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customerid ORDER BY date ASC, time ASC) as rn
    FROM transactions_1k
    WHERE amount < 10
  ) t
  WHERE rn = 1
),
CustomersWithNoIssues AS (
  -- Customers who have no transactions with amount < 10
  SELECT DISTINCT t.customerid
  FROM transactions_1k t
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM transactions_1k t2 
    WHERE t2.customerid = t.customerid AND t2.amount < 10
  )
),
LastValidStationPerCustomer AS (
  -- For customers with no issues, find their last valid transaction station
  SELECT ltps.customerid,
         ltps.gasstationid as last_valid_gasstation
  FROM LastTransactionPerStation ltps
  INNER JOIN CustomersWithNoIssues cn ON ltps.customerid = cn.customerid
  WHERE ltps.amount >= 10
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM LastTransactionPerStation ltps2
    INNER JOIN CustomersWithNoIssues cn2 ON ltps2.customerid = cn2.customerid
    WHERE ltps2.customerid = ltps.customerid
      AND ltps2.amount >= 10
      AND (ltps2.date > ltps.date OR (ltps2.date = ltps.date AND ltps2.time > ltps.time))
  )
)
-- Final selection
SELECT ltps.customerid,
       ltps.transactionid,
       ltps.gasstationid,
       ltps.amount
FROM LastTransactionPerStation ltps
INNER JOIN CustomerIssueStations cis ON ltps.customerid = cis.customerid 
                                    AND ltps.gasstationid = cis.first_issue_gasstation

UNION ALL

SELECT ltps.customerid,
       ltps.transactionid,
       ltps.gasstationid,
       ltps.amount
FROM LastTransactionPerStation ltps
INNER JOIN LastValidStationPerCustomer lvsc ON ltps.customerid = lvsc.customerid 
                                          AND ltps.gasstationid = lvsc.last_valid_gasstation
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 
  FROM CustomerIssueStations cis 
  WHERE cis.customerid = ltps.customerid
)

WITH user_action_sequences AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        action,
        action_time,
        LEAD(action_time) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY action_time) AS next_action_time
    FROM user_actions
),
search_sessions AS (
    SELECT 
        s.user_id,
        s.action_time as search_time,
        s.next_action_time
    FROM user_action_sequences s
    WHERE s.action = 'SEARCH'
),
buy_actions AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        action_time
    FROM user_actions
    WHERE action = 'BUY'
)
SELECT 
    ss.user_id,
    COUNT(*) as abandoned_search_count
FROM search_sessions ss
LEFT JOIN buy_actions ba 
    ON ss.user_id = ba.user_id 
    AND ba.action_time > ss.search_time 
    AND (ba.action_time < ss.next_action_time OR ss.next_action_time IS NULL)
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM user_actions ua 
    WHERE ua.user_id = ss.user_id 
    AND ua.action = 'LOGIN' 
    AND ua.action_time > ss.search_time 
    AND (ua.action_time < ss.next_action_time OR ss.next_action_time IS NULL)
)
AND (ba.user_id IS NULL)
GROUP BY ss.user_id

SELECT 
    begin_date, 
    end_date,
    CASE 
        WHEN date_trunc('month', begin_date) = begin_date 
             AND (date_trunc('month', end_date) + interval '1 month - 1 day')::date = end_date
        THEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM age(date_trunc('month', end_date), date_trunc('month', begin_date))) * 12 + 
             EXTRACT(MONTH FROM age(date_trunc('month', end_date), date_trunc('month', begin_date)))
        WHEN date_trunc('month', begin_date) = begin_date
        THEN GREATEST(0, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM age(date_trunc('month', end_date), date_trunc('month', begin_date))) * 12 + 
                      EXTRACT(MONTH FROM age(date_trunc('month', end_date), date_trunc('month', begin_date))) - 1)
        WHEN (date_trunc('month', end_date) + interval '1 month - 1 day')::date = end_date
        THEN GREATEST(0, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM age(date_trunc('month', end_date), date_trunc('month', begin_date))) * 12 + 
                      EXTRACT(MONTH FROM age(date_trunc('month', end_date), date_trunc('month', begin_date))) - 1)
        ELSE GREATEST(0, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM age(date_trunc('month', end_date), date_trunc('month', begin_date))) * 12 + 
                      EXTRACT(MONTH FROM age(date_trunc('month', end_date), date_trunc('month', begin_date))) - 2)
    END AS full_calendar_months
FROM race_dates

WITH player_changes AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id, 
        updated_at,
        MAX(CASE WHEN column_id = 'height' THEN value::text::integer END) AS height,
        MAX(CASE WHEN column_id = 'weight' THEN value::text::integer END) AS weight
    FROM player_stats_changes 
    GROUP BY entity_id, updated_at
),
ordered_changes AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        updated_at,
        height,
        weight,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY updated_at) as rn
    FROM player_changes
),
filled_data AS (
    SELECT 
        entity_id,
        updated_at,
        COALESCE(height, 
            LAST_VALUE(height) OVER (
                PARTITION BY entity_id 
                ORDER BY rn 
                RANGE BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
                EXCLUDE CURRENT ROW
            )
        ) AS height,
        COALESCE(weight, 
            LAST_VALUE(weight) OVER (
                PARTITION BY entity_id 
                ORDER BY rn 
                RANGE BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
                EXCLUDE CURRENT ROW
            )
        ) AS weight
    FROM ordered_changes
)
SELECT 
    entity_id,
    COALESCE(height, 
        (SELECT height FROM filled_data fd2 
         WHERE fd2.entity_id = filled_data.entity_id 
         AND fd2.height IS NOT NULL 
         AND fd2.updated_at <= filled_data.updated_at 
         ORDER BY fd2.updated_at DESC 
         LIMIT 1)
    ) AS height,
    COALESCE(weight, 
        (SELECT weight FROM filled_data fd2 
         WHERE fd2.entity_id = filled_data.entity_id 
         AND fd2.weight IS NOT NULL 
         AND fd2.updated_at <= filled_data.updated_at 
         ORDER BY fd2.updated_at DESC 
         LIMIT 1)
    ) AS weight,
    updated_at
FROM filled_data
ORDER BY entity_id, updated_at

with qr1(item, subgroup) as (
    select id, subgroup1 
    from superhero_group 
    where subgroup1 is not null
), 
qr2(item, subgroup) as (
    select id, subgroup2 
    from superhero_group 
    where subgroup2 is not null
),
combined as (
    select item, subgroup from qr1
    union
    select item, subgroup from qr2
),
-- Find connected components using recursive CTE
components as (
    -- Anchor: start with each item-subgroup pair
    select 
        item,
        subgroup,
        cast(item as varchar(1000)) as component_items,
        item as root_item
    from combined
    
    union all
    
    -- Recursive part: connect items that share subgroups
    select 
        c.item,
        c.subgroup,
        cast(comp.component_items || ',' || c.item as varchar(1000)),
        comp.root_item
    from combined c
    join components comp on c.subgroup = comp.subgroup and c.item != comp.item
),
-- Get distinct components by finding minimum item in each connected component
distinct_components as (
    select 
        item,
        min(root_item) as group_id
    from components
    group by item
)
select 
    sg.id as item,
    sg.subgroup1,
    sg.subgroup2,
    dc.group_id
from superhero_group sg
left join distinct_components dc on sg.id = dc.item
order by dc.group_id, sg.id

SELECT 
    u.row_id AS id,
    u.utm_campaign,
    u.utm_source,
    u.utm_medium,
    u.utm_content,
    u.utm_term,
    r.date_of_visit,
    SUM(r.sessions) AS sessions,
    SUM(r.pageviews) AS pageviews,
    SUM(r.bounces) AS bounces
FROM utm u
JOIN report r ON u.row_id = r.id
    AND (u.utm_campaign = r.utm_campaign OR (u.utm_campaign IS NULL AND r.utm_campaign IS NULL))
    AND (u.utm_source = r.utm_source OR (u.utm_source IS NULL AND r.utm_source IS NULL))
    AND (u.utm_medium = r.utm_medium OR (u.utm_medium IS NULL AND r.utm_medium IS NULL))
    AND (u.utm_content = r.utm_content OR (u.utm_content IS NULL AND r.utm_content IS NULL) OR (u.utm_content IS NULL AND r.utm_content = ''))
    AND (u.utm_term = r.utm_term OR (u.utm_term IS NULL AND r.utm_term IS NULL) OR (u.utm_term IS NULL AND r.utm_term = ''))
GROUP BY 
    u.row_id,
    u.utm_campaign,
    u.utm_source,
    u.utm_medium,
    u.utm_content,
    u.utm_term,
    r.date_of_visit
ORDER BY 
    u.row_id,
    r.date_of_visit

ranked_races AS (
    SELECT 
        gender_id,
        race_id,
        superhero_count,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY gender_id ORDER BY superhero_count DESC) as top_rank,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY gender_id ORDER BY superhero_count ASC) as bottom_rank
    FROM race_counts
)
SELECT 
    gender_id,
    race_id,
    superhero_count,
    CASE 
        WHEN top_rank <= 5 THEN 'TOP_5'
        WHEN bottom_rank <= 5 THEN 'BOTTOM_5'

    bottom_rank

WHERE top_rank <= 5 OR bottom_rank <= 5
ORDER BY gender_id, top_rank, bottom_rank

WITH view_pairs AS (
    SELECT 
        v1.session_id,
        v1.post_id,
        v1.ts AS start_time,
        v2.ts AS end_time,
        v2.view_perc
    FROM view_logs v1
    JOIN view_logs v2 ON v1.session_id = v2.session_id 
                     AND v1.post_id = v2.post_id
                     AND v1.event_name = 'view_start'
                     AND v2.event_name = 'view_end'
                     AND v1.ts < v2.ts
    WHERE v2.ts = (
        SELECT MIN(ts) 
        FROM view_logs v3 
        WHERE v3.session_id = v1.session_id 
          AND v3.post_id = v1.post_id 
          AND v3.event_name = 'view_end' 
          AND v3.ts > v1.ts
    )
)
SELECT 
    session_id,
    post_id,
    start_time,
    end_time,
    CASE 
        WHEN (end_time - start_time > 3 AND view_perc > 0.8) 
        THEN true 
        ELSE false 
    END AS has_impact
FROM view_pairs

SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS yesterday_revenue,
    ROUND(
        (SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) - 
         SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 8 THEN amount ELSE 0 END)) * 100.0 / 
        NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date = '2022-01-27'::date - 8 THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 0), 
        2
    ) AS pct_change_week_ago,
    SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', '2022-01-27'::date) AND timestamp::date <= '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS mtd,
    ROUND(
        (SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', '2022-01-27'::date) AND timestamp::date <= '2022-01-27'::date - 1 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) - 
         SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', '2022-01-27'::date - INTERVAL '1 month') AND timestamp::date <= DATE_TRUNC('month', '2022-01-27'::date) - INTERVAL '1 day' THEN amount ELSE 0 END)) * 100.0 / 
        NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN timestamp::date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', '2022-01-27'::date - INTERVAL '1 month') AND timestamp::date <= DATE_TRUNC('month', '2022-01-27'::date) - INTERVAL '1 day' THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 0), 
        2
    ) AS pct_change_month_prior
FROM transactions

WITH parent_posts AS (
    SELECT 
        p1.id as parent_id,
        p1.score as parent_score,
        p1.acceptedanswerid as child_id,
        p2.score as child_score
    FROM posts p1
    LEFT JOIN posts p2 ON p1.acceptedanswerid = p2.id
    WHERE p1.acceptedanswerid IS NOT NULL
),
qualified_posts AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN parent_score >= 20 THEN parent_id
            WHEN parent_score < 20 AND child_score >= 20 THEN child_id
            ELSE NULL
        END as selected_post_id
    FROM parent_posts
    WHERE (parent_score >= 20) OR (parent_score < 20 AND child_score >= 20)
)
SELECT p.id, p.acceptedanswerid, p.posttypeid, p.score
FROM posts p
JOIN qualified_posts qp ON p.id = qp.selected_post_id

WITH card_activity AS (
    SELECT 
        to_date(fe.start_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD') AS start_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN fe.end_date_key IS NULL THEN NULL 
            ELSE to_date(fe.end_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD') 
        END AS end_date,
        dp.set_name,
        dp.set_code 
    FROM fact_collection fe 
    INNER JOIN dim_set dp ON fe.set_key = dp.set_key
),
months AS (
    SELECT generate_series(
        date_trunc('month', (SELECT MIN(to_date(start_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD')) FROM fact_collection)),
        date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE),
        INTERVAL '1 month'
    ) AS month_series
)
SELECT 
    m.month_series AS month,
    COUNT(ca.*) AS existing_cards,
    ca.set_name
FROM months m
LEFT JOIN card_activity ca ON (
    ca.start_date < m.month_series + INTERVAL '1 month' 
    AND (ca.end_date IS NULL OR ca.end_date >= m.month_series)
)
GROUP BY m.month_series, ca.set_name
ORDER BY m.month_series, ca.set_name

WITH ordered_missions AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id, 
        mission_start, 
        mission_end,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY hero_id ORDER BY mission_start) as rn,
        LAG(mission_end) OVER (PARTITION BY hero_id ORDER BY mission_start) as prev_end
    FROM superhero_missions
),
span_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        mission_start,
        mission_end,
        SUM(CASE WHEN prev_end = mission_start THEN 0 ELSE 1 END) 
            OVER (PARTITION BY hero_id ORDER BY mission_start) as span_group
    FROM ordered_missions
)
SELECT 
    hero_id,
    MIN(mission_start) as mission_start,

    SELECT id as league_id, name, country_id
    FROM league 
    WHERE id = 1  -- Replace with the specific league ID you're interested in
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: since there are no sub-leagues in this schema,

    -- as a template for future use
    SELECT l.id, l.name, l.country_id
    FROM league l

WITH RECURSIVE crafting_costs AS (
    -- Base case: direct costs (resources only, no item dependencies)
    SELECT 
        item_id,
        item_price as retail_price,
        COALESCE(resource_price * quantity, 0) as component_cost,
        uses_item_id,
        0 as level
    FROM store 
    WHERE resource_id IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: handle item dependencies
    SELECT 
        s.item_id,
        s.item_price as retail_price,
        COALESCE(c.component_cost * s.quantity, 0) as component_cost,
        s.uses_item_id,
        c.level + 1
    FROM store s
    JOIN crafting_costs c ON s.uses_item_id = c.item_id
    WHERE s.uses_item_id IS NOT NULL AND c.level < 10  -- Prevent infinite recursion
),
partial_crafting AS (
    -- Calculate partial craft price (resources only)
    SELECT 
        item_id,
        item_price as retail_price,
        SUM(COALESCE(resource_price * quantity, 0)) as partial_craft_price
    FROM store 
    WHERE resource_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY item_id, item_price
),
full_crafting AS (
    -- Calculate full craft price (including nested item costs)
    SELECT 
        item_id,
        item_price as retail_price,
        SUM(component_cost) as full_craft_price
    FROM crafting_costs
    GROUP BY item_id, item_price
)
SELECT 
    COALESCE(p.item_id, f.item_id) as item_id,
    COALESCE(p.retail_price, f.retail_price) as retail_price,
    COALESCE(p.partial_craft_price, 0) as partial_craft_price,
    COALESCE(f.full_craft_price, 0) as full_craft_price
FROM partial_crafting p
FULL OUTER JOIN full_crafting f ON p.item_id = f.item_id
ORDER BY item_id

                    ORDER BY c.id
                ) 
                FROM classes AS c 
                JOIN classyears AS cy2 ON c.id = cy2.classid

WITH RECURSIVE EmpHierarchy AS (
    -- Anchor: Start with all employees and their direct relationships
    SELECT 
        e.id as employee_id,
        e.salary as employee_salary,
        e.bossid as manager_id,
        1 as level
    FROM staff e
    WHERE e.bossid IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive: Continue up the chain to find indirect managers
    SELECT 
        eh.employee_id,
        eh.employee_salary,
        m.bossid as manager_id,
        eh.level + 1 as level
    FROM EmpHierarchy eh
    INNER JOIN staff m ON eh.manager_id = m.id
    WHERE m.bossid IS NOT NULL
),
QualifiedManagers AS (
    -- Find managers who earn at least twice as much as their subordinates
    SELECT 
        eh.employee_id,
        eh.employee_salary,
        eh.level as hierarchical_level,
        m.id as boss_id,
        m.salary as boss_salary
    FROM EmpHierarchy eh
    INNER JOIN staff m ON eh.manager_id = m.id
    WHERE m.salary >= 2 * eh.employee_salary
)
-- Get the lowest-ranked (minimum level) qualified manager for each employee
SELECT 
    employee_id as employeeId,
    employee_salary as employeeSalary,
    hierarchical_level,
    boss_id as bossId,
    boss_salary as bossSalary
FROM QualifiedManagers qm
WHERE qm.hierarchical_level = (
    SELECT MIN(hierarchical_level) 
    FROM QualifiedManagers qm2 
    WHERE qm2.employee_id = qm.employee_id
)
ORDER BY employee_id

WITH RECURSIVE bucketed_transfers AS (
    -- Base case: first row
    SELECT 
        id,
        amount,
        amount as cum_sum,
        1 as bucket
    FROM transfer 
    WHERE id = (SELECT MIN(id) FROM transfer)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: subsequent rows
    SELECT 
        t.id,
        t.amount,
        CASE 
            WHEN bt.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN t.amount
            ELSE bt.cum_sum + t.amount 
        END as cum_sum,
        CASE 
            WHEN bt.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN bt.bucket + 1
            ELSE bt.bucket 
        END as bucket
    FROM transfer t
    JOIN bucketed_transfers bt ON t.id = bt.id + 1
    WHERE t.id <= (SELECT MAX(id) FROM transfer)
)
SELECT id, amount, cum_sum, bucket
FROM bucketed_transfers
ORDER BY id

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to() 
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Update eff_to for the team of the newly inserted record
    UPDATE team_attributes 
    SET eff_to = subquery.next_date 
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            id,
            COALESCE(
                LEAD(TO_DATE(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE, 1) 
                OVER (
                    PARTITION BY team_api_id 
                    ORDER BY TO_DATE(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE
                ),
                TO_DATE('5999-12-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ) - 1 AS next_date
        FROM team_attributes 
        WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
    ) AS subquery
    WHERE team_attributes.id = subquery.id;
    
    RETURN NULL;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER after_insert_team_attributes 
AFTER INSERT ON team_attributes 
FOR EACH ROW 
EXECUTE PROCEDURE update_team_attributes_eff_to()

WITH driver_position_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        driverid,
        position,
        COUNT(*) as race_count
    FROM results 
    WHERE position IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY driverid, position
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
),
position_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        position,
        ARRAY_AGG(driverid) as driverids,
        MAX(driverid) as max_driverid
    FROM driver_position_counts
    GROUP BY position
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT DISTINCT dpc.driverid
FROM driver_position_counts dpc
JOIN position_groups pg ON dpc.position = pg.position
WHERE dpc.driverid < pg.max_driverid
ORDER BY dpc.driverid

WITH RECURSIVE leaf_to_root AS (
    -- Start with all nodes, including their current color
    SELECT id, parent_id, color
    FROM superhero2
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursively propagate colors from children to parents
    SELECT 
        p.id,
        p.parent_id,
        CASE 
            -- Count how many children have propagated colors
            WHEN COUNT(c.id) = 1 THEN MAX(c.color)  -- One child: take its color
            WHEN COUNT(c.id) = 2 AND COUNT(DISTINCT c.color) = 1 THEN MAX(c.color)  -- Two children, same color: take that color
            WHEN COUNT(c.id) = 2 AND COUNT(DISTINCT c.color) = 2 THEN 'gray'  -- Two children, different colors: gray
            ELSE p.color  -- No change if conditions don't match
        END AS color
    FROM superhero2 p
    INNER JOIN leaf_to_root c ON p.id = c.parent_id
    WHERE p.color IS NULL OR p.color = ''  -- Only process nodes that need coloring
    GROUP BY p.id, p.parent_id, p.color
),
final_colors AS (
    -- Get the final color for each node
    SELECT DISTINCT id, color
    FROM leaf_to_root
    WHERE color IS NOT NULL
)
UPDATE superhero2 
SET color = final_colors.color 
FROM final_colors 
WHERE superhero2.id = final_colors.id

WITH artist_totals AS (
    SELECT artist, COUNT(*) as total_cards
    FROM cards
    WHERE artist IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY artist
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
),
artist_set_counts AS (
    SELECT c.artist, c.setcode, COUNT(*) AS set_count
    FROM cards c
    INNER JOIN artist_totals at ON c.artist = at.artist
    WHERE c.artist IS NOT NULL AND c.setcode IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY c.artist, c.setcode
),
max_sets_per_artist AS (
    SELECT artist, MAX(set_count) as max_count
    FROM artist_set_counts
    GROUP BY artist
)
SELECT 
    asc.artist,
    asc.setcode,
    asc.set_count as counter,
    CASE WHEN asc.set_count = mspa.max_count THEN 'HIGHEST' ELSE '' END as highest_set
FROM artist_set_counts asc
INNER JOIN max_sets_per_artist mspa ON asc.artist = mspa.artist
INNER JOIN artist_totals at ON asc.artist = at.artist

INSERT INTO hero_attribute_bifurcation (hero_id, attribute_id, attribute_chunk, is_bifurcated)
WITH bifurcated_data AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        attribute_value,
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN 50
            ELSE attribute_value
        END AS chunk_value,
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END AS is_split,
        generate_series(1, 
            CASE 
                WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN ceil(attribute_value::decimal / 50)::integer
                ELSE 1
            END
        ) AS chunk_num
    FROM hero_attribute
),
expanded_data AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        chunk_value,
        is_split,
        chunk_num,
        attribute_value,
        CASE 
            WHEN chunk_num = ceil(attribute_value::decimal / 50)::integer 
             AND attribute_value % 50 != 0 
             AND attribute_value > 50
            THEN attribute_value % 50
            WHEN chunk_num < ceil(attribute_value::decimal / 50)::integer 
             AND attribute_value > 50
            THEN 50
            ELSE chunk_value
        END AS final_chunk_value
    FROM bifurcated_data
)
SELECT 
    hero_id,
    attribute_id,
    final_chunk_value AS attribute_chunk,
    is_split AS is_bifurcated
FROM expanded_data
ORDER BY hero_id, attribute_id, chunk_num

FROM (

WITH activity_groups AS (
  SELECT 
    ts,
    superhero_id,
    activity_code,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY superhero_id, activity_code, ts) -
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) AS grp
  FROM superhero_activities
),
grouped_intervals AS (
  SELECT 
    superhero_id,
    activity_code,
    MIN(ts) AS start_time,
    MAX(ts) AS end_time,
    COUNT(*) AS interval_count
  FROM activity_groups
  GROUP BY superhero_id, activity_code, grp
)
SELECT 
  superhero_id,
  activity_code,
  start_time,
  end_time,
  interval_count,
  EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (end_time - start_time))/300 + 1 AS total_intervals -- assuming 5-minute intervals
FROM grouped_intervals
ORDER BY superhero_id, activity_code, start_time

WITH RECURSIVE hierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: root nodes (no parent)
    SELECT
        uuid,
        card_name,
        parent_uuid,
        0 as level,
        uuid::text as path
    FROM card_type
    WHERE parent_uuid IS NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: child nodes
    SELECT
        c.uuid,
        c.card_name,
        c.parent_uuid,
        h.level + 1,
        h.path || '.' || c.uuid::text
    FROM card_type c
    JOIN hierarchy h ON c.parent_uuid = h.uuid
),
-- Get direct children for each parent
parent_with_children AS (
    SELECT 
        p.uuid as parent_uuid,
        p.card_name as parent_name,
        COALESCE(
            json_agg(
                json_build_object(
                    'uuid', c.uuid,
                    'card_name', c.card_name
                )
            ) FILTER (WHERE c.uuid IS NOT NULL),
            '[]'
        ) as children
    FROM card_type p
    LEFT JOIN card_type c ON c.parent_uuid = p.uuid
    GROUP BY p.uuid, p.card_name
)
SELECT 
    parent_uuid as uuid,
    parent_name as card_name,
    children
FROM parent_with_children
ORDER BY parent_name

SELECT

    FROM generate_series(timestamp '2020-01-01', timestamp '2020-03-31', interval '1 day') ts
) d 
LEFT JOIN "event" e ON DATE(e.event_date) = d.the_day AND e.status IN ('Open', 'Closed')
LEFT JOIN budget b ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id 
GROUP BY d.the_day 
ORDER BY d.the_day

SELECT

    SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM trans.date) = 1999 THEN trans.amount ELSE 0 END) AS a1999
FROM trans 
WHERE trans.date >= '1997-01-01' AND trans.date < '2000-01-01'
GROUP BY month 
ORDER BY month

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION words_get_chat(in_gid integer, in_social integer, in_sid text) RETURNS TABLE (out_mine integer, out_msg text) AS $func$ 
SELECT 
    CASE WHEN c.uid = s.uid THEN 1 ELSE 0 END, 
    c.msg 
FROM words_chat c 
JOIN words_games g USING (gid) 
JOIN words_social s ON s.uid IN (g.player1, g.player2) 
JOIN words_users u ON u.uid = c.uid
WHERE c.gid = in_gid 
    AND s.social = in_social 
    AND s.sid = in_sid 
    AND (u.muted = false OR c.uid = s.uid)
ORDER BY c.CREATED ASC; 
$func$ LANGUAGE sql

WITH RECURSIVE result_calc AS (
    -- Base case: first row
    SELECT 
        id,
        trx,
        event,
        ROUND((0 + trx) * event, 6) AS result_good,
        1 AS row_num
    FROM sales_transactions 
    WHERE id = (SELECT MIN(id) FROM sales_transactions)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: subsequent rows
    SELECT 
        st.id,
        st.trx,
        st.event,
        ROUND((rc.result_good + st.trx) * st.event, 6) AS result_good,
        rc.row_num + 1
    FROM sales_transactions st
    JOIN result_calc rc ON st.id = (
        SELECT MIN(id) 
        FROM sales_transactions 
        WHERE id > rc.id
    )
)
SELECT id, result_good AS result
FROM result_calc
ORDER BY id

WITH cte_funding_count AS (
    SELECT fundingtype, COUNT(*) AS count 
    FROM schools 
    GROUP BY fundingtype
),
cte_with_percentage AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype, 
        count,
        count * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(count) FROM cte_funding_count) AS exact_percent,
        ROUND(count * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(count) FROM cte_funding_count), 0) AS rounded_percent,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY (count * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(count) FROM cte_funding_count)) - FLOOR(count * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(count) FROM cte_funding_count)) DESC) AS rn
    FROM cte_funding_count
),
cte_total_check AS (
    SELECT SUM(rounded_percent) AS total_percent FROM cte_with_percentage
)
SELECT 
    fundingtype,
    count,
    CAST(
        CASE 
            WHEN rn <= (100 - (SELECT total_percent FROM cte_total_check)) THEN rounded_percent + 1
            ELSE rounded_percent
        END AS INTEGER
    ) AS percent
FROM cte_with_percentage
ORDER BY count DESC

SELECT first_lab.firstActivity, first_lab.id
FROM (
    SELECT lab.date AS firstActivity, pat.id,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY pat.id ORDER BY lab.date ASC) as rn
    FROM patient pat 
    JOIN laboratory lab ON lab.id = pat.id 
    WHERE pat.sex = 'F' 
      AND pat.description IS NOT NULL
      AND lab.date >= pat."First Date" 
      AND lab.date < pat.description
) first_lab
WHERE first_lab.rn = 1
ORDER BY first_lab.id

SELECT 
    c.id,
    CASE 
        WHEN array_agg(
            elem ORDER BY elem
        ) FILTER (
            WHERE elem <> ALL(
                COALESCE(sq.removekeywords, ARRAY[]::text[])
            )
        ) IS NULL THEN ARRAY[]::text[]
        ELSE array_agg(
            elem ORDER BY elem
        ) FILTER (
            WHERE elem <> ALL(
                COALESCE(sq.removekeywords, ARRAY[]::text[])
            )
        )
    END AS keywords
FROM cards c
JOIN selectquery sq ON c.id = sq.id
CROSS JOIN unnest(
    string_to_array(
        trim(both '{}' FROM c.keywords), 
        ','
    )::text[]
) AS elem
WHERE c.id IN (61, 65)
GROUP BY c.id
ORDER BY c.id

        )) = TRIM(BOTH ' ' FROM REGEXP_REPLACE(
            COALESCE(circui_name_b1, '') || ' ' ||

WITH trading_days AS (
    -- Get distinct card_id and trading days
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        card_id,
        DATE(date_value) as trade_date
    FROM card_prices
),
first_prices AS (
    -- Get the first price of each trading day for each card
    SELECT 
        cp.card_id,
        td.trade_date,
        MIN(cp.date_value) as first_timestamp,
        cp.price as first_price
    FROM card_prices cp
    JOIN trading_days td ON cp.card_id = td.card_id 
        AND DATE(cp.date_value) = td.trade_date
    GROUP BY cp.card_id, td.trade_date
),
fifteen_min_prices AS (
    -- Get the last price within 15 minutes of the first price for each card/day
    SELECT 
        fp.card_id,
        fp.trade_date,
        fp.first_price,
        fp.first_timestamp,
        MAX(cp.date_value) as last_timestamp_within_15min
    FROM first_prices fp
    JOIN card_prices cp ON fp.card_id = cp.card_id 
        AND DATE(cp.date_value) = fp.trade_date
        AND cp.date_value >= fp.first_timestamp
        AND cp.date_value <= fp.first_timestamp + INTERVAL '15 minutes'
    GROUP BY fp.card_id, fp.trade_date, fp.first_price, fp.first_timestamp
),
price_differences AS (
    -- Get the actual prices and calculate differences
    SELECT 
        fp.card_id,
        fp.trade_date,
        fp.first_price,
        cp.price as last_price,
        (cp.price - fp.first_price) as price_difference
    FROM first_prices fp
    JOIN fifteen_min_prices fmp ON fp.card_id = fmp.card_id 
        AND fp.trade_date = fmp.trade_date
    JOIN card_prices cp ON fp.card_id = cp.card_id 
        AND cp.date_value = fmp.last_timestamp_within_15min
)
SELECT 
    card_id,
    AVG(price_difference) as avg_difference
FROM price_differences
GROUP BY card_id
ORDER BY card_id

create or replace function setup_vector_store(table_name text) returns void language plpgsql as $outer$ 
begin 
    -- Drop any existing match function for this specific table
    execute format('drop function if exists match_%I(int, jsonb)', table_name);
    
    -- Create the strategies table with vector column
    execute format('create table %I ( 
        id bigint primary key generated always as identity, 
        description text, 
        metadata jsonb,
        embedding vector(1536)
    )', table_name);
    
    -- Create the matching function for this table
    execute format('create function match_%I(
        query_embedding vector(1536),
        match_count int default null, 
        filter jsonb default ''{}''::jsonb
    ) returns table (
        id bigint, 
        description text, 
        metadata jsonb, 
        similarity float
    ) language plpgsql as $inner$
    begin 
        return query 
        select 
            s.id, 
            s.description, 
            s.metadata, 
            1 - (s.embedding <=> query_embedding) as similarity
        from %I s
        where s.metadata @> filter 
        order by s.embedding <=> query_embedding 
        limit match_count; 
    end; 
    $inner$;', table_name, table_name);
end; 
$outer$

WITH product_attributes AS (
    SELECT 
        dp.id as datapoint_id,
        MAX(CASE WHEN var.name = 'ProductType' THEN vv.name END) AS product_type,
        MAX(CASE WHEN var.name = 'publishing_language' THEN vv.name END) AS language,
        MAX(CASE WHEN var.name = 'genre' THEN vv.name END) AS genre
    FROM datapoints dp
    JOIN datapoint_variablevalue dpvv ON dp.id = dpvv.datapoint_id
    JOIN variablevalue vv ON dpvv.variablevalue_id = vv.id
    JOIN variable var ON vv.variable_id = var.id
    GROUP BY dp.id
)
SELECT 
    COALESCE(SUM(dp.value), 0) AS total_sales,
    td.years AS year,
    sd.name AS country,
    COALESCE(pa.product_type, 'Unknown') AS product_type,
    COALESCE(pa.language, 'Unknown') AS language,
    COALESCE(pa.genre, 'Unknown') AS genre
FROM time_dimension td
CROSS JOIN spatial_dimension sd
CROSS JOIN (
    SELECT DISTINCT product_type, language, genre 
    FROM product_attributes
) pa_combinations
LEFT JOIN spatial_dimension sd_lookup ON sd_lookup.name = sd.name
LEFT JOIN time_dimension td_lookup ON td_lookup.years = td.years
LEFT JOIN datapoints dp ON dp.time_dimension_id = td.id AND dp.spatial_dimension_id = sd.id
LEFT JOIN product_attributes pa ON pa.datapoint_id = dp.id
GROUP BY td.years, sd.name, pa.product_type, pa.language, pa.genre
ORDER BY td.years DESC, sd.name, pa.product_type, pa.language, pa.genre

ORDER BY 
    pg_relation_size(schemaname || '.' || tablename) DESC
LIMIT 3

WITH RECURSIVE split_events AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    UNNEST(STRING_TO_ARRAY(event_description, '#')) AS token,
    GENERATE_SUBSCRIPTS(STRING_TO_ARRAY(event_description, '#'), 1) AS position
  FROM match_events
),
common_tokens AS (
  SELECT 
    position,
    token,
    COUNT(*) AS token_count,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM match_events) AS total_events
  FROM split_events
  GROUP BY position, token
  HAVING COUNT(*) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM match_events)
),
prefix_positions AS (
  SELECT position
  FROM generate_series(1, (SELECT MAX(position) FROM common_tokens))
  WHERE position IN (SELECT position FROM common_tokens)
  ORDER BY position
),
consecutive_check AS (
  SELECT 
    position,
    position - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY position) AS grp
  FROM prefix_positions
),
valid_prefix_length AS (
  SELECT MIN(position) - 1 AS valid_length
  FROM (
    SELECT generate_series AS position
    FROM generate_series(1, (SELECT COALESCE(MAX(position), 0) FROM split_events))
    EXCEPT
    SELECT DISTINCT position
    FROM common_tokens
  ) missing
  WHERE position > 0
)
SELECT 
  CASE 
    WHEN COUNT(*) = 0 THEN ''
    ELSE (
      SELECT STRING_AGG(token, '#' ORDER BY position)
      FROM (
        SELECT DISTINCT se.position, se.token
        FROM split_events se
        JOIN common_tokens ct ON se.position = ct.position AND se.token = ct.token
        WHERE se.position <= COALESCE((SELECT valid_length FROM valid_prefix_length WHERE valid_length > 0), 
                                    (SELECT MAX(position) FROM common_tokens))
        ORDER BY se.position
        LIMIT (SELECT COALESCE((SELECT valid_length FROM valid_prefix_length WHERE valid_length > 0), 
                              (SELECT MAX(position) FROM common_tokens)))
      ) sub
    )
  END AS common_prefix
FROM match_events

-- First, create a regular unique index for the basic uniqueness
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX match_results_match_team_unique_idx ON match_results (match_id, team_id);

-- Create a trigger function to enforce score consistency
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION enforce_score_consistency()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Check if there are existing records with same match_id and team_id but different scores
    IF EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 
        FROM match_results 
        WHERE match_id = NEW.match_id 
        AND team_id = NEW.team_id 
        AND score != NEW.score
    ) THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Score inconsistency: All records with same match_id and team_id must have the same score';
    END IF;
    
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Create the trigger
CREATE TRIGGER trigger_enforce_score_consistency
    BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON match_results
    FOR EACH ROW
    EXECUTE FUNCTION enforce_score_consistency()

WITH monthly_data AS (
  SELECT 
    rate,
    LOWER(time_span)::DATE AS start_date,
    CASE 
      WHEN UPPER(time_span) IS NULL THEN NOW()::DATE
      ELSE (UPPER(time_span) - INTERVAL '1 day')::DATE
    END AS end_date
  FROM budgets
  WHERE LOWER(time_span) IS NOT NULL
),
expanded_dates AS (
  SELECT 
    rate,
    generate_series(start_date, end_date, '1 day')::DATE AS day_date
  FROM monthly_data
)
SELECT 
  day_date,
  ROUND(rate / EXTRACT(DAYS FROM (DATE_TRUNC('month', day_date) + INTERVAL '1 month - 1 day')), 2) AS daily_rate
FROM expanded_dates
ORDER BY day_date

grouped_labs AS (

           count(id) as total_tests

DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS update_school_inventory_after_supply() CASCADE;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_school_inventory_after_supply() 
RETURNS TRIGGER 
LANGUAGE plpgsql 
AS $trigger$ 
BEGIN 
    -- Update existing inventory record or insert new one if it doesn't exist
    INSERT INTO school_inventory (cdscode, supply_name, quantity)
    VALUES (NEW.cdscode, NEW.supply_name, NEW.quantity)
    ON CONFLICT (cdscode, supply_name) 
    DO UPDATE SET 
        quantity = school_inventory.quantity + NEW.quantity,
        last_updated = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP;
    
    RETURN NEW; 
END; 
$trigger$;

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trigger_insert_supply ON school_supplies;

CREATE TRIGGER trigger_insert_supply 
AFTER INSERT ON school_supplies 
FOR EACH ROW 
EXECUTE FUNCTION update_school_inventory_after_supply()

    GROUP BY county 
    ORDER BY avg_total_sat DESC 
    LIMIT 5
)
SELECT s.county, s.sname, s.dname, s.avgscrread, s.avgscrmath, s.avgscrwrite,
       (s.avgscrread + s.avgscrmath + s.avgscrwrite) / 3 AS school_avg_total_sat,
       tc.avg_total_sat AS county_avg_total_sat

WHERE s.avgscrread IS NOT NULL AND s.avgscrmath IS NOT NULL AND s.avgscrwrite IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY tc.avg_total_sat DESC, school_avg_total_sat DESC

    cr.curriculum_id,
    ARRAY_LENGTH(ARRAY(
        SELECT DISTINCT unnest(
            ARRAY_AGG(e."Content_Type") ||

GROUP BY cr.curriculum_id

-- 1) Add a "status" column to curricula
ALTER TABLE curricula 
  ADD COLUMN status TEXT DEFAULT 'installed';

-- 2) Corrected recursive CTE to find all dependent curricula
WITH RECURSIVE dependent_curricula AS (
    -- Base case: start with the specific curriculum to upgrade
    SELECT c.curriculum_id
    FROM curricula c
    WHERE c.curriculum_id = 1   -- e.g., "Introduction to Programming"

    UNION

    -- Recursive step: find curricula that share resources with already identified curricula
    SELECT DISTINCT cr2.curriculum_id
    FROM curriculum_resources cr1
    JOIN curriculum_resources cr2 
         ON cr1.resource_key = cr2.resource_key 
         AND cr1.curriculum_id != cr2.curriculum_id
    JOIN dependent_curricula dc 
         ON cr1.curriculum_id = dc.curriculum_id
    WHERE cr2.curriculum_id NOT IN (SELECT curriculum_id FROM dependent_curricula)
)
UPDATE curricula
SET status = 'to upgrade'
WHERE curriculum_id IN (
    SELECT curriculum_id 
    FROM dependent_curricula
)

WITH RECURSIVE time_segments AS (
  -- Base case: start with the original event intervals
  SELECT 
    id,
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    start_timestamp,
    end_timestamp,
    duration,
    start_timestamp as current_start,
    end_timestamp as current_end
  FROM event_usage
  WHERE start_timestamp BETWEEN '2024-10-01' AND '2024-10-09'
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive case: split intervals that cross day boundaries
  SELECT 
    id,
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    start_timestamp,
    end_timestamp,
    duration,
    DATE_TRUNC('day', current_start) + INTERVAL '1 day' as current_start,
    current_end
  FROM time_segments
  WHERE DATE_TRUNC('day', current_start) + INTERVAL '1 day' <= current_end
),
working_hours_calculation AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    current_start,
    current_end,
    -- Calculate the working hours portion for each segment
    CASE 
      -- If it's a weekend, no working hours
      WHEN EXTRACT(DOW FROM current_start) IN (0, 6) THEN 0
      ELSE
        -- Calculate overlap with working hours (9:00-18:00)
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM 
          LEAST(current_end, DATE_TRUNC('day', current_start) + INTERVAL '18 hours') -
          GREATEST(current_start, DATE_TRUNC('day', current_start) + INTERVAL '9 hours')
        )
    END AS working_seconds
  FROM time_segments
  WHERE 
    -- Only consider segments where there's some overlap with working hours
    EXTRACT(DOW FROM current_start) NOT IN (0, 6) AND
    current_end > DATE_TRUNC('day', current_start) + INTERVAL '9 hours' AND
    current_start < DATE_TRUNC('day', current_start) + INTERVAL '18 hours'
)
SELECT 
  learner_code, 
  resource_key, 
  SUM(GREATEST(0, working_seconds)) AS total_time_spent
FROM working_hours_calculation
GROUP BY learner_code, resource_key
ORDER BY learner_code, resource_key

WITH rated_data AS (
  SELECT 
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    usage_date,
    rating,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY learner_code, resource_key ORDER BY usage_date) as rn
  FROM resource_usage_2
),
forward_fill AS (
  SELECT 
    r1.learner_code,
    r1.resource_key,
    r1.usage_date,
    r1.rating,
    r1.rn,
    MAX(CASE WHEN r2.rating IS NOT NULL THEN r2.rn END) OVER (
      PARTITION BY r1.learner_code, r1.resource_key 
      ORDER BY r1.rn 
      ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) as last_rated_rn
  FROM rated_data r1
  LEFT JOIN rated_data r2 ON r1.learner_code = r2.learner_code 
                          AND r1.resource_key = r2.resource_key 
                          AND r2.rating IS NOT NULL 
                          AND r2.rn <= r1.rn
),
final_result AS (
  SELECT 
    f.learner_code,
    f.resource_key,
    f.usage_date,
    f.rating,
    COALESCE(f.rating, r.rating) as filled_rating
  FROM forward_fill f
  LEFT JOIN rated_data r ON f.learner_code = r.learner_code 
                         AND f.resource_key = r.resource_key 
                         AND f.last_rated_rn = r.rn
)
SELECT 
  learner_code,
  resource_key,
  usage_date,
  rating,
  filled_rating
FROM final_result
ORDER BY learner_code, resource_key, usage_date

WITH date_series AS (
    SELECT generate_series::date AS day_date
    FROM generate_series('2022-01-01'::date, '2022-01-31'::date, '1 day'::interval)
),
incident_day_overlap AS (
    SELECT 
        ds.day_date,
        ri.incident_start,
        ri.incident_end,
        -- Calculate the start of the day and end of the day
        ds.day_date::timestamp AS day_start,
        (ds.day_date + interval '1 day' - interval '1 second')::timestamp AS day_end,
        -- Find the actual overlap period
        GREATEST(ri.incident_start, ds.day_date::timestamp) AS overlap_start,
        LEAST(ri.incident_end, (ds.day_date + interval '1 day' - interval '1 second')::timestamp) AS overlap_end
    FROM date_series ds
    CROSS JOIN race_incidents ri
    WHERE ri.incident_start IS NOT NULL 
      AND ri.incident_end IS NOT NULL
      AND ri.incident_start <= (ds.day_date + interval '1 day' - interval '1 second')::timestamp
      AND ri.incident_end >= ds.day_date::timestamp
)
SELECT 
    day_date,
    COALESCE(SUM(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (overlap_end - overlap_start)) / 60), 0) AS incident_minutes
FROM incident_day_overlap
GROUP BY day_date
ORDER BY day_date

WITH ranked_exams AS (

        e.id,
        e.ldh,
        e.alp,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY e.id ORDER BY e.date) as rn
    FROM examination e
),
exam_pivot AS (
    SELECT 
        p.id,
        MAX(CASE WHEN re.rn = 1 THEN re.ldh END) as ldh_1,
        MAX(CASE WHEN re.rn = 1 THEN re.alp END) as alp_1,
        MAX(CASE WHEN re.rn = 2 THEN re.ldh END) as ldh_2,
        MAX(CASE WHEN re.rn = 2 THEN re.alp END) as alp_2,
        MAX(CASE WHEN re.rn = 3 THEN re.ldh END) as ldh_3,
        MAX(CASE WHEN re.rn = 3 THEN re.alp END) as alp_3
    FROM patient p
    LEFT JOIN ranked_exams re ON p.id = re.id
    GROUP BY p.id
)
SELECT 
    p.id,
    p.sex,
    p.birthday,
    p.diagnosis as patient_diagnosis,
    ep.ldh_1,
    ep.alp_1,
    ep.ldh_2,
    ep.alp_2,
    ep.ldh_3,
    ep.alp_3
FROM patient p
LEFT JOIN exam_pivot ep ON p.id = ep.id
ORDER BY p.id

    FLOOR((EXTRACT(DAY FROM date) - 1 + 
           EXTRACT(DOW FROM DATE_TRUNC('month', date))) / 7) AS admin_week_number

CREATE TABLE loan (

    -- other loan columns would go here
    amount DECIMAL,
    duration INTEGER,
    status TEXT
);

WITH updated_rows AS (
    UPDATE loan 
    SET status = 'updated_status',
        date = 'new_date',
        amount = 'new_amount'
    WHERE loan_id = 'loan_id_value' 
    AND status = 'current_status'
    RETURNING loan_id, status
),
check_existence AS (
    SELECT loan_id 
    FROM loan 
    WHERE loan_id = 'loan_id_value'
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM updated_rows) > 0 THEN 'successful update'
        WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM check_existence) > 0 THEN 'version conflict'
        ELSE 'loan not found'
    END AS outcome

FROM

    WHERE NOT EXISTS (

        AND (a2.hide_in_online_scheduling = FALSE OR a2.hide_in_online_scheduling IS NULL)
    )
    GROUP BY p.id
)

WITH RECURSIVE inventory_matching AS (
  -- Base case: Get all buy transactions with running totals
  SELECT 
    molecule_id,
    transaction_id,
    date,
    molecules as original_molecules,
    molecules as remaining_molecules,
    usd_total,
    usd_total / molecules as unit_price,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date) as buy_order
  FROM transaction 
  WHERE sell = FALSE
),
sales_with_allocation AS (
  -- Get all sell transactions with row numbers
  SELECT 
    molecule_id,
    transaction_id,
    date,
    molecules as sell_molecules,
    usd_total as sell_usd_total,
    usd_total / molecules as sell_unit_price,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date) as sell_order
  FROM transaction 
  WHERE sell = TRUE
),
allocation_steps AS (
  -- Recursive part to match sales with purchases
  SELECT 
    s.molecule_id,
    s.transaction_id as sell_transaction_id,
    s.date as sell_date,
    s.sell_molecules,
    s.sell_unit_price,
    s.sell_order,
    b.transaction_id as buy_transaction_id,
    b.date as buy_date,
    b.unit_price,
    CASE 
      WHEN b.remaining_molecules >= s.sell_molecules 
      THEN s.sell_molecules 
      ELSE b.remaining_molecules 
    END as allocated_molecules,
    CASE 
      WHEN b.remaining_molecules >= s.sell_molecules 
      THEN s.sell_molecules * b.unit_price
      ELSE b.remaining_molecules * b.unit_price
    END as buy_usd_value,
    s.sell_molecules * s.sell_unit_price as sell_usd_value,
    CASE 
      WHEN b.remaining_molecules > s.sell_molecules 
      THEN b.remaining_molecules - s.sell_molecules
      ELSE 0
    END as new_remaining_molecules,
    CASE 
      WHEN b.remaining_molecules > s.sell_molecules 
      THEN b.buy_order
      ELSE b.buy_order + 1
    END as next_buy_order,
    1 as step
  FROM sales_with_allocation s
  JOIN inventory_matching b ON s.molecule_id = b.molecule_id 
    AND b.buy_order = 1
  WHERE s.sell_order = 1
  
  UNION ALL
  
  SELECT 
    s.molecule_id,
    s.transaction_id,
    s.date,
    s.sell_molecules,
    s.sell_unit_price,
    s.sell_order,
    b.transaction_id,
    b.date,
    b.unit_price,
    CASE 
      WHEN b.remaining_molecules >= s.sell_molecules 
      THEN s.sell_molecules 
      ELSE b.remaining_molecules 
    END,
    CASE 
      WHEN b.remaining_molecules >= s.sell_molecules 
      THEN s.sell_molecules * b.unit_price
      ELSE b.remaining_molecules * b.unit_price
    END,
    s.sell_molecules * s.sell_unit_price,
    CASE 
      WHEN b.remaining_molecules > s.sell_molecules 
      THEN b.remaining_molecules - s.sell_molecules
      ELSE 0
    END,
    CASE 
      WHEN b.remaining_molecules > s.sell_molecules 
      THEN b.buy_order
      ELSE b.buy_order + 1
    END,
    a.step + 1
  FROM sales_with_allocation s
  JOIN allocation_steps a ON s.molecule_id = a.molecule_id 
    AND s.sell_order = a.sell_order + 1
  JOIN inventory_matching b ON s.molecule_id = b.molecule_id 
    AND b.buy_order = a.next_buy_order
)
SELECT 
  molecule_id,
  buy_date,
  sell_date,
  allocated_molecules as molecules,
  buy_usd_value as buy_usd_total,
  sell_usd_value as sell_usd_total
FROM allocation_steps
WHERE allocated_molecules > 0
ORDER BY molecule_id, sell_date, buy_date

SELECT 
    patients.patient_id, 
    MIN(treatment_and_followup.primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score) AS min_performance_score
FROM patients 
JOIN treatment_and_followup ON patients.patient_id = treatment_and_followup.patient_id 
WHERE patients.informed_consent_verified = TRUE 
    AND treatment_and_followup.primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY patients.patient_id

WITH ordered_data AS (
    SELECT 
        prediction_code,
        Performance_Prediction,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY prediction_code) as rn
    FROM performance_predictions
),
grouped_data AS (
    SELECT 
        prediction_code,
        Performance_Prediction,
        rn,
        1 as group_num,
        Performance_Prediction as running_sum
    FROM ordered_data
    WHERE rn = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        o.prediction_code,
        o.Performance_Prediction,
        o.rn,
        CASE 
            WHEN g.running_sum + o.Performance_Prediction > 200 
            THEN g.group_num + 1 
            ELSE g.group_num 
        END,
        CASE 
            WHEN g.running_sum + o.Performance_Prediction > 200 
            THEN o.Performance_Prediction
            ELSE g.running_sum + o.Performance_Prediction 
        END
    FROM ordered_data o
    JOIN grouped_data g ON o.rn = g.rn + 1
)
SELECT 
    group_num,
    MAX(running_sum) as cumulative_sum
FROM grouped_data
GROUP BY group_num
ORDER BY group_num

SELECT

    customerid,

    productid, 
    amount,

        ELSE 0

FROM transactions_1k

SELECT DISTINCT A.client_id AS child 
FROM client A 
INNER JOIN client B ON A.district_id = B.district_id
WHERE A.birth_date > B.birth_date + INTERVAL '16 years'
ORDER BY A.client_id

SELECT DISTINCT c1.client_id
FROM client c1
JOIN client c2 ON c1.district_id = c2.district_id 
    AND c1.client_id != c2.client_id
WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM AGE(c1.birth_date)) <= EXTRACT(YEAR FROM AGE(c2.birth_date)) - 16
ORDER BY c1.client_id

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN c.type = 'gold' THEN 'Gold' 
        ELSE 'Non-Gold' 
    END AS card_type_group, 
    COUNT(l.loan_id) AS loan_count 
FROM loan l 
JOIN account a ON l.account_id = a.account_id
JOIN disp d ON a.account_id = d.account_id
JOIN card c ON d.disp_id = c.disp_id 
GROUP BY 
    CASE 
        WHEN c.type = 'gold' THEN 'Gold' 
        ELSE 'Non-Gold' 
    END

WITH attendance_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        COUNT(*) AS attendance_count
    FROM attendance
    GROUP BY link_to_member, link_to_event
),
top_events_per_member AS (
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        attendance_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY link_to_member ORDER BY attendance_count DESC) as member_rank
    FROM attendance_counts
),
top_member_per_event AS (
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        attendance_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY link_to_event ORDER BY attendance_count DESC) as event_rank
    FROM attendance_counts
)
SELECT 
    link_to_member,
    link_to_event,
    attendance_count
FROM top_events_per_member
WHERE member_rank <= 2
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    link_to_member,
    link_to_event,
    attendance_count
FROM top_member_per_event
WHERE event_rank = 1
ORDER BY link_to_member, link_to_event

    'top_stations_per_customer' as query_type
FROM transactions_1k 
WHERE customerid IS NOT NULL AND gasstationid IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY customerid, gasstationid
ORDER BY customerid, attendance_count DESC
LIMIT 2147483647) -- This will get all results, then we'll limit per customer

UNION ALL

-- Top 1 customer per gas station based on transaction count
(SELECT 
    gasstationid,
    customerid,
    COUNT(*) as attendance_count,
    'top_customer_per_station' as query_type
FROM transactions_1k 
WHERE customerid IS NOT NULL AND gasstationid IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY gasstationid, customerid
ORDER BY gasstationid, attendance_count DESC
LIMIT 2147483647) -- This will get all results, then we'll limit per station

-- First, create a new backup table with the merged data
WITH merged_data AS (
    -- Get all rows from source table
    SELECT * FROM treatment_and_followup
    UNION ALL
    -- Get rows from target table that don't exist in source table
    SELECT t.* FROM treatment_and_followup_latest t
    LEFT JOIN treatment_and_followup s ON t.patient_id = s.patient_id
    WHERE s.patient_id IS NULL
)
-- Replace the backup table with merged data
DELETE FROM treatment_and_followup_backup;

INSERT INTO treatment_and_followup_backup 
SELECT * FROM merged_data;

-- Update the latest table to match the source (synchronize)
DELETE FROM treatment_and_followup_latest;

INSERT INTO treatment_and_followup_latest 
SELECT * FROM treatment_and_followup

SELECT 
    learner_code, 
    CASE 
        WHEN Geographic_Location = 'Urban' THEN 1 
        WHEN Geographic_Location = 'Rural' THEN 2 
        ELSE 0 
    END AS location_id, 
    AVG(Pre_Test_Score) AS average_pre_test_score 
FROM learners 
JOIN outcomes USING (learner_code)
WHERE 
    Engagement_History > 5 
    AND Gender = 'Female' 
    AND Language_Proficiency IN ('Basic', 'Intermediate')
    AND (
        (${socio_status} IN ('Low', 'Middle') AND Geographic_Location = 'Urban') OR
        (${socio_status} = 'High' AND Geographic_Location = 'Rural')
    )
GROUP BY learner_code, Geographic_Location

WITH src(id,geom) as (values 
    (1, 'linestring(0 0, 0 10)'::geometry), 
    (2, 'linestring(0 10, 0 11)'::geometry), 
    (3, 'linestring(1 1, 0 5)'::geometry), 
    (4, 'linestring(1 1, 2 2)'::geometry)
)
SELECT DISTINCT a.id, st_asText(a.geom) as geometry
FROM src AS a 
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM src as b 
    WHERE a.id != b.id 
      AND ST_Intersects(a.geom, b.geom)
      AND NOT ST_Touches(a.geom, b.geom)
)

    device_info::json ->> 'SerialNumber' AS SerialNumber,
    (cto ->> 'ChannelId')::INT AS ChannelId,
    cto ->> 'Name' AS ChannelName
FROM device_details 
CROSS JOIN LATERAL json_array_elements(device_info::json -> 'ChannelListDto') AS Channels(cto)

WITH date_list AS (
  SELECT generate_series(
    DATE '2023-01-01',
    DATE '2023-01-10',
    '1 day'::interval
  )::date AS calendar_date
),
business_dates AS (
  SELECT calendar_date
  FROM date_list
  WHERE EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM calendar_date) < 6  -- Exclude Saturday (6) and Sunday (7)
),
business_time_calc AS (
  SELECT 
    COUNT(*) * INTERVAL '24 hours' AS total_interval
  FROM business_dates
)
SELECT 
  EXTRACT(DAY FROM total_interval) || ' days ' ||
  EXTRACT(HOUR FROM total_interval) || ' hours ' ||
  EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM total_interval) || ' minutes ' ||
  EXTRACT(SECOND FROM total_interval) || ' seconds ' AS time_diff
FROM business_time_calc

WITH links AS (
    SELECT 
        greatest(source_cluster, target_cluster) AS big, 
        least(source_cluster, target_cluster) AS smol, 
        type 
    FROM link
),
tag_pairs AS (
    SELECT 
        l.cluster AS left_id, 
        l.cluster_type AS left_type, 
        l.cluster_label AS left_label, 
        r.cluster AS right_id, 
        r.cluster_type AS right_type, 
        r.cluster_label AS right_label,
        l.article
    FROM tag l
    JOIN tag r ON l.article = r.article 
    WHERE l.cluster > r.cluster
)
SELECT 
    tp.left_id,
    tp.left_type,
    tp.left_label,
    tp.right_id,
    tp.right_type,
    tp.right_label,
    COUNT(DISTINCT tp.article) AS articles,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT l.type) AS link_types
FROM tag_pairs tp
LEFT JOIN links l ON tp.left_id = l.big AND tp.right_id = l.smol
GROUP BY 
    tp.left_id, 
    tp.left_type, 
    tp.left_label, 
    tp.right_id, 
    tp.right_type, 
    tp.right_label
ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT tp.article) DESC

WITH ranked_schools AS (
    SELECT 
        county,
        opendate,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY county ORDER BY opendate) as rn
    FROM schools
    WHERE opendate IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    county,
    MIN(CASE WHEN rn = 1 THEN opendate END) AS earliest_open_date,
    MIN(CASE WHEN rn = 4 THEN opendate END) AS fourth_open_date
FROM ranked_schools
WHERE rn <= 4
GROUP BY county
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 4
ORDER BY county

WITH max_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        inst."Institution_Type",
        MAX(outcm."Pre_Test_Score") as max_pre_test_score
    FROM outcomes as outcm 
    JOIN enrollment as enrl ON outcm.learner_code = enrl.learner_code 
    JOIN institutions as inst ON enrl.institution_key = inst.institution_key 
    WHERE inst."Institution_Type" IN ('Public', 'Private')
    GROUP BY inst."Institution_Type"
)
SELECT 
    (SELECT max_pre_test_score FROM max_scores WHERE "Institution_Type" = 'Public') -
    (SELECT max_pre_test_score FROM max_scores WHERE "Institution_Type" = 'Private') as score_diff

SELECT 
    CASE WHEN is_other_method THEN NULL ELSE primary_pathology_neoplasm_histologic_grade END AS histologic_grade,
    CASE WHEN is_other_method THEN NULL ELSE primary_pathology_days_to_initial_pathologic_diagnosis END AS days_to_initial_diagnosis,
    CASE WHEN is_other_method THEN NULL ELSE primary_pathology_age_at_initial_pathologic_diagnosis END AS age_at_initial_diagnosis
FROM (
    SELECT 
        *,

                 primary_pathology_init_pathology_dx_method_other) LIKE 'Other method, specify:') AS is_other_method

) AS subquery

WITH consolidated_periods AS (
    SELECT 
        alb,
        art,
        feat_arts,
        MIN(start_date) as start_date,
        MAX(end_date) as end_date
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            alb,
            art,
            feat_arts,
            start_date,
            end_date,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY alb, art, feat_arts ORDER BY start_date) -
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY alb, art, feat_arts ORDER BY start_date RANGE BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) as grp
        FROM sp_album
    ) grouped
    GROUP BY alb, art, feat_arts, grp
)
SELECT 
    alb,
    art,
    feat_arts,
    start_date,
    CASE 
        WHEN end_date IS NULL THEN NULL
        WHEN LEAD(start_date) OVER (PARTITION BY alb, art, feat_arts ORDER BY start_date) <= end_date + INTERVAL '1 day' THEN NULL
        ELSE end_date
    END as end_date
FROM consolidated_periods
ORDER BY alb, art, start_date

WITH sales AS ( 
    SELECT "gasstationid" AS "GasstationId", 
           "customerid" AS "CustomerId", 
           "date" AS "SaleDate", 
           "amount" AS "Amount" 
    FROM "transactions_1k" 
), 
initial_purchase AS ( 
    SELECT "GasstationId", 
           "CustomerId", 
           MIN("SaleDate") AS "FirstPurchaseDate" 
    FROM sales 
    GROUP BY "GasstationId", "CustomerId" 
), 
customer_wages AS ( 
    SELECT s."CustomerId", 
           s."GasstationId",
           s."SaleDate",
           s."Amount"
    FROM sales s 
    JOIN initial_purchase i 
        ON s."CustomerId" = i."CustomerId" 
        AND s."GasstationId" = i."GasstationId"
    WHERE s."SaleDate" >= i."FirstPurchaseDate" 
    ORDER BY s."CustomerId" DESC 
) 
SELECT * FROM customer_wages

SELECT DISTINCT all_dates.transaction_date, all_salespeople.salesperson_name
FROM (SELECT DISTINCT transaction_date FROM sales_log) all_dates
CROSS JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT salesperson_name FROM sales_log) all_salespeople
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM sales_log sl
    WHERE sl.transaction_date = all_dates.transaction_date

SELECT DISTINCT p.product_id, p.product_name
FROM product p
JOIN productprice pp_current ON p.product_id = pp_current.product_id
WHERE pp_current.effective_from = (
    SELECT MAX(effective_from)
    FROM productprice
    WHERE product_id = p.product_id
)
AND pp_current.price < (
    SELECT pp_prev.price
    FROM productprice pp_prev
    WHERE pp_prev.product_id = p.product_id
    AND pp_prev.effective_from = (
        SELECT MAX(effective_from)
        FROM productprice
        WHERE product_id = p.product_id
        AND effective_from < pp_current.effective_from
    )
)
ORDER BY p.product_id

         split_part(zone, '-', 2)::int >= 10015)

SELECT 
  CASE 
    WHEN HOUR(Description) > 0 AND MINUTE(Description) > 0 AND SECOND(Description) > 0 THEN
      CONCAT(HOUR(Description), ' hours, ', MINUTE(Description), ' minutes, ', SECOND(Description), ' seconds')
    WHEN HOUR(Description) > 0 AND MINUTE(Description) > 0 THEN
      CONCAT(HOUR(Description), ' hours, ', MINUTE(Description), ' minutes')
    WHEN HOUR(Description) > 0 AND SECOND(Description) > 0 THEN
      CONCAT(HOUR(Description), ' hours, ', SECOND(Description), ' seconds')
    WHEN MINUTE(Description) > 0 AND SECOND(Description) > 0 THEN
      CONCAT(MINUTE(Description), ' minutes, ', SECOND(Description), ' seconds')
    WHEN HOUR(Description) > 0 THEN
      CONCAT(HOUR(Description), ' hours')
    WHEN MINUTE(Description) > 0 THEN
      CONCAT(MINUTE(Description), ' minutes')
    WHEN SECOND(Description) > 0 THEN
      CONCAT(SECOND(Description), ' seconds')
    ELSE '0 seconds'
  END AS formatted_duration
FROM Patient

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN Count < 1000 THEN 'Under 1000'
        WHEN Count BETWEEN 1000 AND 9999 THEN '1000 to 9999'
        WHEN Count BETWEEN 10000 AND 49999 THEN '10000 to 49999'
        WHEN Count >= 50000 THEN '50000 and above'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
    END AS range,
    AVG(Count) AS avg_count
FROM tags
WHERE Count IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 
    CASE 
        WHEN Count < 1000 THEN 'Under 1000'
        WHEN Count BETWEEN 1000 AND 9999 THEN '1000 to 9999'
        WHEN Count BETWEEN 10000 AND 49999 THEN '10000 to 49999'
        WHEN Count >= 50000 THEN '50000 and above'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
    END
ORDER BY 
    MIN(Count)

SELECT 
    id,
    skill_value,
    skill_name
FROM (
    SELECT 
        id,
        skill_value,
        skill_name,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY id ORDER BY skill_value DESC) as rn
    FROM (
        SELECT id, crossing AS skill_value, 'crossing' AS skill_name FROM Player_Attributes
        UNION ALL
        SELECT id, finishing AS skill_value, 'finishing' AS skill_name FROM Player_Attributes
        UNION ALL
        SELECT id, heading_accuracy AS skill_value, 'heading_accuracy' AS skill_name FROM Player_Attributes
        UNION ALL
        SELECT id, short_passing AS skill_value, 'short_passing' AS skill_name FROM Player_Attributes
        UNION ALL
        SELECT id, volleys AS skill_value, 'volleys' AS skill_name FROM Player_Attributes

SELECT cr.release_id, cr.code_id, cr.code_status_id, cr.code_created_date
FROM card_release cr
WHERE cr.code_id IS NOT NULL 
  AND cr.code_status_id IN (2, 3)
  AND (
    -- Select 'paid' records (priority 1)
    cr.code_status_id = 3
    OR 
    -- For 'requesting' records, only select if there's no 'paid' record for the same voucher
    (cr.code_status_id = 2 
     AND NOT EXISTS (
       SELECT 1 
       FROM card_release cr2 
       WHERE cr2.code_id = cr.code_id 
         AND cr2.code_status_id = 3
     )
     AND cr.code_created_date = (
       SELECT MAX(cr3.code_created_date)
       FROM card_release cr3
       WHERE cr3.code_id = cr.code_id
         AND cr3.code_status_id = 2
     )
    )
  )

WITH RECURSIVE EmissionHierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: leaf nodes (entities that are not parents of any other entity)
    SELECT 
        entityid,
        parentid,
        emission,
        emission AS cumulative_total,
        0 AS level
    FROM tree_table 
    WHERE entityid NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT parentid FROM tree_table WHERE parentid IS NOT NULL)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: propagate cumulative totals upward
    SELECT 
        t.entityid,
        t.parentid,
        t.emission,
        t.emission + (eh.cumulative_total * eh.pctcontribution / 100.0) AS cumulative_total,
        eh.level + 1
    FROM tree_table t
    INNER JOIN EmissionHierarchy eh ON t.entityid = eh.parentid
)
SELECT 
    entityid,
    parentid,
    ROUND(cumulative_total, 2) AS cumulative_total
FROM EmissionHierarchy
ORDER BY entityid

  SELECT 1 AS num
  UNION ALL
  SELECT num + 1
  FROM numbers
  WHERE num < (SELECT COALESCE(MAX(id), 0) + 100 FROM (SELECT id FROM Worker1 UNION SELECT id FROM Worker2) combined)
),
combined_workers AS (
  SELECT id FROM Worker1
  UNION
  SELECT id FROM Worker2
)

FROM numbers n
LEFT JOIN combined_workers cw ON n.num = cw.id
WHERE cw.id IS NULL
AND n.num > 0

WITH OverlappingPromotions AS (
  SELECT 
    p1.PRODUCT,
    p1.DISCOUNT AS DISCOUNT1,
    p1.DISCOUNT_START AS START1,
    p1.DISCOUNT_END AS END1,
    p2.DISCOUNT AS DISCOUNT2,
    p2.DISCOUNT_START AS START2,
    p2.DISCOUNT_END AS END2
  FROM promotions p1
  JOIN promotions p2 
    ON p1.PRODUCT = p2.PRODUCT
    AND p1.ROWID < p2.ROWID
  WHERE p1.DISCOUNT_START <= p2.DISCOUNT_END 
    AND p2.DISCOUNT_START <= p1.DISCOUNT_END
)
SELECT DISTINCT PRODUCT
FROM OverlappingPromotions

WITH consecutive_pairs AS (
  SELECT 
    p1.product_name AS first_product,
    p2.product_name AS second_product,
    COUNT(*) AS pair_count
  FROM orders p1 
  JOIN orders p2 ON p1.customer_id = p2.customer_id 
  WHERE p1.purchase_rank + 1 = p2.purchase_rank
  GROUP BY p1.product_name, p2.product_name
),
total_pairs AS (
  SELECT SUM(pair_count) AS grand_total
  FROM consecutive_pairs
)
SELECT 
  first_product,
  second_product,
  pair_count,
  ROUND((pair_count * 100.0 / grand_total), 2) AS percentage
FROM consecutive_pairs
CROSS JOIN total_pairs
ORDER BY pair_count DESC

SELECT 
    day_name AS day,
    date_start,
    date_end
FROM (
    SELECT 'monday' AS day_name, CAST(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(slot.value, '$.start')) AS TIME) AS date_start, CAST(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(slot.value, '$.end')) AS TIME) AS date_end
    FROM event e, JSON_TABLE(e.event_schedule, '$.monday[*]' COLUMNS(value JSON PATH '$')) AS slot
    WHERE e.event_id = 'rec0Si5cQ4rJRVzd6' AND JSON_EXTRACT(e.event_schedule, '$.monday') IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 'tuesday' AS day_name, CAST(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(slot.value, '$.start')) AS TIME) AS date_start, CAST(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(slot.value, '$.end')) AS TIME) AS date_end
    FROM event e, JSON_TABLE(e.event_schedule, '$.tuesday[*]' COLUMNS(value JSON PATH '$')) AS slot
    WHERE e.event_id = 'rec0Si5cQ4rJRVzd6' AND JSON_EXTRACT(e.event_schedule, '$.tuesday') IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 'wednesday' AS day_name, CAST(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(slot.value, '$.start')) AS TIME) AS date_start, CAST(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(slot.value, '$.end')) AS TIME) AS date_end
    FROM event e, JSON_TABLE(e.event_schedule, '$.wednesday[*]' COLUMNS(value JSON PATH '$')) AS slot
    WHERE e.event_id = 'rec0Si5cQ4rJRVzd6' AND JSON_EXTRACT(e.event_schedule, '$.wednesday') IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 'thursday' AS day_name, CAST(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(slot.value, '$.start')) AS TIME) AS date_start, CAST(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(slot.value, '$.end')) AS TIME) AS date_end
    FROM event e, JSON_TABLE(e.event_schedule, '$.thursday[*]' COLUMNS(value JSON PATH '$')) AS slot
    WHERE e.event_id = 'rec0Si5cQ4rJRVzd6' AND JSON_EXTRACT(e.event_schedule, '$.thursday') IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 'friday' AS day_name, CAST(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(slot.value, '$.start')) AS TIME) AS date_start, CAST(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(slot.value, '$.end')) AS TIME) AS date_end
    FROM event e, JSON_TABLE(e.event_schedule, '$.friday[*]' COLUMNS(value JSON PATH '$')) AS slot
    WHERE e.event_id = 'rec0Si5cQ4rJRVzd6' AND JSON_EXTRACT(e.event_schedule, '$.friday') IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 'saturday' AS day_name, CAST(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(slot.value, '$.start')) AS TIME) AS date_start, CAST(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(slot.value, '$.end')) AS TIME) AS date_end
    FROM event e, JSON_TABLE(e.event_schedule, '$.saturday[*]' COLUMNS(value JSON PATH '$')) AS slot
    WHERE e.event_id = 'rec0Si5cQ4rJRVzd6' AND JSON_EXTRACT(e.event_schedule, '$.saturday') IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 'sunday' AS day_name, CAST(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(slot.value, '$.start')) AS TIME) AS date_start, CAST(JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(slot.value, '$.end')) AS TIME) AS date_end
    FROM event e, JSON_TABLE(e.event_schedule, '$.sunday[*]' COLUMNS(value JSON PATH '$')) AS slot
    WHERE e.event_id = 'rec0Si5cQ4rJRVzd6' AND JSON_EXTRACT(e.event_schedule, '$.sunday') IS NOT NULL
) AS result
ORDER BY FIELD(day, 'monday', 'tuesday', 'wednesday', 'thursday', 'friday', 'saturday', 'sunday'), date_start

    COALESCE(p.Description, '') AS Description
FROM transactions_1k t1
LEFT JOIN gasstations g ON t1.GasStationID = g.GasStationID
LEFT JOIN products p ON t1.ProductID = p.ProductID

UNION
